2604.12460.title: Enhancing Clustering: An Explainable Approach via Filtered Patterns
2604.12460.paper_summary: Machine learning has become a central research area, with increasing attention devoted to explainable clustering, also known as conceptual clustering, which is a knowledge-driven unsupervised learning paradigm that partitions data into $θ$ disjoint clusters, where each cluster is described by an explicit symbolic representation, typically expressed as a closed pattern or itemset. By providing human-interpretable cluster descriptions, explainable clustering plays an important role in explainable artificial intelligence and knowledge discovery. Recent work improved clustering quality by introducing k-relaxed frequent patterns (k-RFPs), a pattern model that relaxes strict coverage constraints through a generalized kcover definition. This framework integrates constraint-based reasoning, using SAT solvers for pattern generation, with combinatorial optimization, using Integer Linear Programming (ILP) for cluster selection. Despite its effectiveness, this approach suffers from a critical limitation: multiple distinct k-RFPs may induce identical k-covers, leading to redundant symbolic representations that unnecessarily enlarge the search space and increase computational complexity during cluster construction. In this paper, we address this redundancy through a pattern reduction framework. Our contributions are threefold. First, we formally characterize the conditions under which distinct k-RFPs induce identical kcovers, providing theoretical foundations for redundancy detection. Second, we propose an optimization strategy that removes redundant patterns by retaining a single representative pattern for each distinct k-cover. Third, we investigate the interpretability and representativeness of the patterns selected by the ILP model by analyzing their robustness with respect to their induced clusters. Extensive experiments conducted on several real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly reduces the pattern search space, improves computational efficiency, preserves and enhances in some cases the quality of the resulting clusters.
2604.12460.paper_summary_zh: 機器學習已成為一個核心研究領域，越來越多的注意力集中在可解釋的聚類上，也稱為概念聚類，這是一種知識驅動的無監督學習範式，將數據劃分為 $θ$ 個不相交的聚類，每個聚類由明確的符號表示來描述，通常表達為封閉模式或項集。通過提供人類可解釋的聚類描述，可解釋的聚類在可解釋的人工智慧和知識發現中扮演著重要角色。最近的研究通過引入 k-放鬆頻繁模式 (k-RFPs) 改進了聚類質量，這是一種通過廣義的 k-cover 定義放鬆嚴格覆蓋約束的模式模型。這個框架整合了基於約束的推理，使用 SAT 解算器進行模式生成，與組合優化，使用整數線性規劃 (ILP) 進行聚類選擇。儘管這種方法有效，但它存在一個關鍵的限制：多個不同的 k-RFPs 可能會引起相同的 k-covers，導致冗餘的符號表示，這不必要地擴大了搜索空間並增加了聚類構建過程中的計算複雜性。在本文中，我們通過一個模式減少框架來解決這一冗餘問題。我們的貢獻有三個方面。首先，我們正式描述了不同的 k-RFPs 產生相同 k-covers 的條件，為冗餘檢測提供理論基礎。其次，我們提出了一種優化策略，通過保留每個不同 k-cover 的單個代表模式來去除冗餘模式。第三，我們通過分析所選模式對其誘導聚類的穩健性，研究 ILP 模型所選模式的可解釋性和代表性。在幾個現實世界數據集上進行的廣泛實驗表明，所提出的方法顯著減少了模式搜索空間，提高了計算效率，在某些情況下保留並增強了結果聚類的質量。
2604.12460.author: Motaz Ben Hassine et.al.
